name: Pear and Maple-Sauced Pork Chops
recipeIngredient: 1 large Pear, ripe, cored and sliced
4 (4 oz) boneless pork loin chops, trimmed of all fat
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium bowl, combine pear, onion, maple-flavored syrup, margarine (butter), and ginger; mix well. Set aside.
2. Sprinkle both sides of pork chops with salt and pepper. Spray heavy nonstick skillet with nonstick cooking spray. Heat over medium-high heat until hot. Add pork chops. Cook 2-3 minutes on each side or until browned. Pour pear mixture over pork chops. Reduce heat to medium-low heat and cover; cook 12-15 minutes or until pork chops are no longer pink.
3. Place pork chops on serving platter; cover to keep warm. Cook sauce over medium-high heat for 3-4 minutes, stirring constantly, or until sauce is thickened. Stir in maple flavor. Spoon sauce over chops.
